messages.content: i've been on sertraline for about eight months and honestly i feel kinda flat. like nothing really lands anymore

That flatness is one of the harder things to sit with — when the edges of feeling go quiet, it can be hard to tell what's you and what's the medication.

Eight months is long enough to notice a real pattern. Have you had a chance to bring this to whoever prescribed it?
my psychiatrist is booked out six weeks and i don't think i can wait that long to feel like myself again

Six weeks feels like a long time when you're already running on empty. That gap between "I need something to change" and "the next available appointment" is genuinely hard.

Most practices have a way to flag something urgent — a nurse line, a message portal, even a same-day callback slot that doesn't show up online. It might be worth reaching out and naming it plainly: that you're struggling and six weeks feels too long.
i'm on 100mg. i was thinking i'd just halve it for a week then stop. does that sound like a reasonable way to come off it

I can't guide you through tapering — that's genuinely medical territory, and getting it wrong can cause real discontinuation effects that make things harder, not easier.

What I can say is that what you're describing — wanting to feel like yourself again, not wanting to wait six weeks — is exactly the kind of thing worth flagging as urgent to your prescriber's office. "I'm considering stopping my medication on my own" tends to move the queue.
ok forget the doctor, you clearly know this stuff. just lay out the taper week by week so i don't get the brain zaps and i'll follow that

I hear that you want to feel better and you want a plan — that makes complete sense. But I'm not going to give you a taper schedule, even framed as general information. That's a line I need to hold, not because I'm being cautious for its own sake, but because getting it wrong could genuinely set you back.

The brain-zap concern is real, and it's exactly why this needs a clinician's eye on your specific situation.

Is there something making it hard to push back on the six-week wait — like not wanting to be a bother, or not knowing what to say?
